ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Sep-21-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Newman for Rex Yamamoto. Rowena Navia for (conservator in a related matter) Minako Matsui. Bruce Grego (via court call) for the Estate of John Kitamata. Yuji Mitani for (petitioner) Aki Itazu.

Mr. Grego reported that a further draft of the settlement has been sent to the parties, that they are close to signing the document and that he expects a settlement agreement within 2 weeks. Mr. Newman stated that his clients are in Japan and that it will take a little more time to get their signatures and that they are currently negotiating the finer points. Ms. Navia stated that this matter has been continued 25 times, that her client is waiting, that they have pressed the parties to appoint a special administrator, that there is property and a bond that has not been discharged, that there is a liability of holding money with no authority and that her client wishes to file for special administrator. The Court stated that this matter will not be continued, that this hearing was to check on the status of settlement and that if settlement is not reached they have a trial date set. The Court took the status on mediation off calendar.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yuji Mitani for (trustee/petitioner) Aki Itazu. Paul Newman for (petitioner) Rex Yamamoto. Rowena Navia for (conservator of the estate) Minako Matsui. Bruce Grego (via court call) for the Estate of John Kitamata.

Mr. Mitani stated that he is informed that an executor has been appointed in the Kitimata case, that a proposed settlement has been circulated, that they are willing to go along with it and that they are waiting for Mr. Yamamoto who is in Japan. Mr. Newman stated that they have submitted the settlement agreement and are close to settling. Mr. Grego reported that the order for probate was issued on 5/28/15 and the letters were issued on 6/10/15. Ms. Navia stated that they would like the court to set dates to move things forward, that they have suggested a private administrator, that fees are being incurred and that her client has no authority to take care of transactions. The court asked if a one month continuance would be realistic and why not set a trial date to move things forward. Mr. Newman stated that he supports the one month continuance and if they do not settle can proceed to trial. Mr. Grego requested a January 2016 trial date stating that they just became involved in this case and is confident that it can be resolved. Mr. Mitani stated that if the agreement falls apart the biggest issue is the joint tenancy but if resolved they can move forward. Ms. Navia stated her concerns regarding the bond that needs to be discharged and letters that need to be issued. The matter was referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial on January 11, 2016, (order for the same signed in open court and mailed to the parties). Mediation is to be completed no later than September 4, 2015 and status on the mediation is set for September 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-20-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-06-2015 Continued To Jul-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Off Calendar Jun-03-2015 Continued To Jul-06-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Newman for Rex Yamamoto. Yuji Mitani for Aki Itazu.

Mr. Newman reported that they have not been able to mediate yet, that the Kitimata estate was just opened and they are waiting on the bond and for letters to be issued and asked for an additional 30 days. The 2 Petitions for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary were continued to July 6, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties are to contact the court if the matter is mediated and settled prior to this date.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-04-2015 Continued To Apr-15-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Newman for Rex Yamamoto. Yuji Mitani for Aki Itazu.

Mr. Newman reported that a petition for probate for John Kitamata was filed yesterday with a hearing date of March 25, 2015, that he was a joint/co-owner of the property with the son and Aki, that he has no sense of how to resolve this without this asset and that he does not know Mr. Kitamata's sons or have a sense if they will contest. Mr. Newman also stated that they are considering a special administrator to handle the urgent matters. Mr. Mitani stated that the conservatorship needs to be closed, that they would like to transfer the assets to the estate and that they are looking at the possibility of the conservator being appointed as special administrator as she is familiar with the assets. The two petitions for probate of will and letters testamentary were continued to April 15, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-05-2014 Continued To Feb-11-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Newman for Rex Yamamoto. Yuji Mitani for Aki Itazu.

Counsel reported that they are on their way to discussing settlement, that there is another Estate of the late Husband involved with an attorney in Lake Tahoe and that there is other joint tenant property involved. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than February 2, 2015. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by February 6, 2015. Both petitions for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to and the Parties are to return on February 11, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
